description_long: This object is a streamlined electric fan with a cylindrical body that tapers toward the rear, resembling a jet engine nacelle. The front features a circular intake with a set of evenly spaced, angled blades radiating from a central hub, designed to maximize airflow. The outer casing is smooth and continuous, with subtle ridges running lengthwise along the top, likely for structural reinforcement or aesthetic appeal. The rear of the fan has a large, open exhaust, allowing for efficient air passage. The entire surface appears to be made from a hard, matte material, possibly molded plastic or metal. There are no visible external controls, power cords, or mounting brackets, giving it a minimalist and modern appearance.
description: A streamlined, cylindrical electric fan with a smooth, hard casing and a circular intake featuring angled blades, resembling a jet engine. The design is minimalist with no visible controls or cords.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular intake and a set of angled fan blades radiating from a central hub.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the smooth, tapered body and the rounded front intake, with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the large, open exhaust and the subtle longitudinal ridges on the casing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the continuous, streamlined surface.
attribute_blade_guard_design: The blade guard is integrated into the front intake, appearing as a solid ring with no mesh or grille, leaving the blades exposed.
